goal: Widen stdlib semantic support in corpus-priority order while preserving strict proof boundaries and avoiding material performance regression.
performance_gate: Each slice should stay within <=5% median wall-clock growth on focused release query measurements, with NOSE_TIME phase output checked for obvious normalize/extract regressions.

slices.capability: Go strings.Join namespace calls canonicalize to the existing ordered Join builtin when the strings package identity is proven by import evidence.
changes: Added the Go strings.Join namespace-call row to nose.go.stdlib.namespace_calls.
Normalized Go strings.Join(parts, sep) into Join(sep, parts), matching the shared join semantic shape.
Kept local values named strings and unsupported string transforms closed.
unsupported_boundaries: strings.Trim, strings.Split, strings.Replace, and case transforms still lack admitted value semantics.
Unproven receivers or local same-name values do not prove the stdlib namespace-call contract.

performance.command: NOSE_TIME=1 target/release/nose query bench/repos/chi --mode semantic --format json --min-value 100000000
NOSE_TIME=1 target/release/nose query bench/repos/gorm --mode semantic --format json --min-value 100000000

slices.capability: Fully-qualified java.util.Optional receivers expose option presence and default-value semantics through existing builtin contracts.
changes: Added Java Optional.isPresent() as IsNotNull on exact Option receivers.
Added Java Optional.orElse(default) as ValueOrDefault on exact Option receivers.
Restricted Java Optional type-domain evidence to java.util.Optional so local or bare Optional names do not prove stdlib semantics without import-backed type proof.
unsupported_boundaries: Bare imported Optional<T> remains closed until Java type-domain evidence can depend on import identity.
Optional.isEmpty(), Optional.of/empty/ofNullable, map, flatMap, filter, orElseGet, and orElseThrow remain unsupported boundaries.

performance.command: NOSE_TIME=1 target/release/nose query bench/repos/commons-lang --mode semantic --format json --min-value 100000000
NOSE_TIME=1 target/release/nose query bench/repos/guava --mode semantic --format json --min-value 100000000

slices.capability: Corpus-level method audit separates already-admitted Java Arrays/Collections factories from copy, equality, mutation, wrapper, and representation boundaries.
changes: Generated a checked-in 120-repo Java Arrays/Collections audit report.
Classified observed unsupported methods by semantic-kernel capability bucket instead of proposing one API row per method.
unsupported_boundaries: Arrays.copyOf/copyOfRange need copy-result domain proof and bounds obligations.
Arrays.equals/deepEquals need array content equality laws.
Arrays.fill/sort and Collections.addAll/sort/reverse/shuffle remain mutation/effect surfaces.
Collections.unmodifiable/synchronized/checked wrappers need alias/lifecycle modeling before admission.

slices.capability: Alias-aware corpus audit separates admitted Go slices.Contains membership from sort/slices/maps mutation, ordering, callback, iterator, copy, and equality boundaries.
changes: Generated a checked-in Go sort/slices/maps audit report across the pinned Go corpus.
Resolved simple import aliases before counting package calls, keeping the report closer to future namespace-proof behavior.
unsupported_boundaries: sort.Sort/Strings/Slice and slices.Sort* are in-place ordering mutations.
sort.Search* and BinarySearch* need sortedness or monotonic predicate preconditions.
slices.ContainsFunc/IndexFunc and SortFunc/CompareFunc need callback identity and effect proof.
maps.Clone/slices.Clone/slices.Concat need copy-result domain and alias/lifetime proof.
